metadata.title: Voila-A: Aligning Vision-Language Models with User's Gaze Attention
metadata.authors: Kun Yan, Lei Ji, Zeyu Wang, Yuntao Wang, Nan Duan, Shuai Ma
metadata.affiliation: Beihang University · Microsoft Research Asia · Tsinghua University

captions.fig_1: Figure 1: AR and VR scenarios usually involve complex scenes with multiple objects. Users may interested in only one specific object and gaze is the most natural way to interact with the device.
captions.fig_2: Figure 2: EMD between the mean heatmaps of 1k gaze and trace samples with varying sampling rates.
captions.fig_7: Figure 7: Different Scenarios in the Future.
captions.fig_8: Figure 8: Qualitative Case Study. Top: We show successful predictions of all models. Middle: We demonstrate the problems of baseline models compared with VOILA including coreference queries, gaze grounding methods, etc. Bottom: We display hard challenges for all models.
captions.table_1: Table 1: Statistics of Voila-COCO and Voila-Gaze Datasets.
captions.table_3: Table 3: Ablation on Methods of Integrating Gaze Data.
